creator: beebeetles@posteo.de
text:
It appears that hw.snd.default_unit can be modified by any unprivileged user, including those in a jailed environment. This behavior can potentially lead to  unintended consequences. 

Suppose we have two sound devices /dev/dsp1 and /dev/dsp2 on the system, with default_unit set to 1, and /dev/dsp2 exposed to a jail through devfs(8). Since a jailed process is free to modify the value of hw.snd.default_unit, it can set its value to 2, thus directing to itself audio recordings that is originally intended for the host environment (or other jails).

Similarly in a non-jailed environment, attempts to control access to /dev/dspN devices though permission bits can fail for the same reason. Unprivileged user can work around the access control by modifying the default unit to the one that they are given access to.
